title: Veradermics (MANE) Reports Q4 Net Loss of $21.8M
content:
Veradermics, Incorporated (NYSE:MANE) is one of the

8 Healthcare Stocks Insiders Are Buying. On March 30, 2026, Veradermics, Incorporated (NYSE:MANE) reported a Q4 net loss of $21.8M and ended the year with $141.9M in cash, cash equivalents, and marketable securities. CEO Reid Waldman said 2025 was a “landmark year,” highlighting progress in Phase 3 development of VDPHL01 and the company’s IPO, while noting expectations for two Phase 3 readouts in men, continued progress toward an NDA submission, and advancement of a Phase 3 trial for female pattern hair loss.

Earlier in March, Cantor Fitzgerald analyst Prakhar Agrawal initiated coverage on Veradermics, Incorporated (NYSE:MANE) with an Overweight rating, noting the company’s focus on dermatology and its use of “validated biology” of minoxidil with optimized pharmacokinetics to improve efficacy and safety.Veradermics (MANE) Reports Q4 net loss of $21.8M

Copyright: nyul / 123RF Stock Photo

Similarly, Leerink initiated coverage on Veradermics, Incorporated (NYSE:MANE) with an Outperform rating and a $75 price target, highlighting the large market for hair loss treatments and positioning VDPHL01 as a potential best-in-class oral therapy for both men and women, supported by a hybrid marketing strategy combining telehealth and direct access.

Veradermics, Incorporated (NYSE:MANE) develops therapies for dermatologic and aesthetic conditions.

While we acknowledge the potential of MANE as an investment, we believe certain AI stocks offer greater upside potential and carry less downside risk. If you're looking for an extremely undervalued AI stock that also stands to benefit significantly from Trump-era tariffs and the onshoring trend, see our free report on the best short-term AI stock.

title: Catalyst Watch: Jobs report, new Apple products, and a spotlight on software
content:
[New York Stock Exchange]
Selcuk Oner/iStock Editorial via Getty Images

Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

SUNDAY - MARCH 1

 	* ALL DAY - Eight key OPEC+ nations will hold their monthly video conference and decide on production levels for April. 
 	* DATA WATCH - Key data reports expected to arrive include deliveries reports from Nio (NIO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NIO]), XPeng (XPEV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XPEV]), Li Auto (LI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LI]), and BYD Company (BYDDF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BYDDF]), as well as the monthly Macau gross gaming revenue release.

MONDAY - MARCH 2

 	* VOLATILITY WATCH - AST SpaceMobile (ASTS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ASTS]) and Xenon Pharmaceuticals (XENE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XENE]) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include ATI Inc. (ATI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ATI]), Keysight (KEYS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/KEYS]), and Nordic American Tankers (NAT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NAT]). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Gossamer Bio (GOSS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GOSS]), FuboTV (FUBO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FUBO]), and Oddity Tech (ODD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ODD]). Short interest is elevated on software stocks UiPath (PATH [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PATH]), Duolingo (DUOL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DUOL]), Coreweave (CRWV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CRWV]), and RingCentral (RNG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RNG]).
 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Progressive (PGR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PGR]), MongoDB (MDB [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MDB]), Riot Platforms (RIOT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RIOT]), and EchoStar (SATS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SATS]). Options trading implies a double-digit swing in share price for AST SpaceMobile (ASTS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ASTS]) and Asana (ASAN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ASAN]) after they report.
 	* DIVIDEND WATCH - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Allstate (ALL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ALL]), Lam Research (LRCX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LRCX]), Cigna (CI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CI]), and Qualcomm (QCOM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/QCOM]).
 	* IPO WATCH - MiniMed Group (MMED [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MMED]) will start trading after being separated from Medtronic (MDT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MDT]), which will still own about 90% of the company. The analyst quiet period expires on Forgent Power Solutions (FPS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FPS]), Eikon Therapeutics (EIKN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/EIKN]), Bob’s Discount Furniture (BOBS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BOBS]), and VeraDermics (MANE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MANE]) to free up analysts to post ratings.
 	* ALL WEEK - The Department of Justice antitrust trial against Live Nation–Ticketmaster (LYV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LYV]) will take place.
 	* ALL DAY - The three-day Morgan Stanley Technology, Media & Telecom Conference in San Francisco will begin. Some of the notable companies participating include Walmart (WMT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WMT]), Cisco (CSCO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CSCO]), Disney (DIS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DIS]), Comcast (CMCSA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CMCSA]), Expedia (EXPE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/EXPE]), Amdocs (DOX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DOX]), Autodesk (ADSK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ADSK]), NetApp (NTAP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NTAP]), and Carvana (CVNA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CVNA]).
 	* ALL DAY - The Mobile World Congress Barcelona event will begin. Notable companies participating include Cisco (CSCO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CSCO]), Qualcomm (QCOM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/QCOM]), AT&T (T [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/T]), and SpaceX (SPACE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SPACE]).
 	* ALL DAY - DraftKings (DKNG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DKNG]) will also host a virtual Investor Day. Management is expected to share views on the opportunity ahead, outline the company’s sustainable advantages, and discuss financial framework and capital allocation priorities.

TUESDAY - MARCH 3

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Crowdstrike (CRWD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CRWD]), Ross Stores (ROST [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ROST]), Autozone (AZO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AZO]), Target (TGT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TGT]), Best Buy (BBY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BBY]), On Holding (ONON [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ONON]), and Box (BOX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BOX]). Options trading implies a double-digit swing in share price for Sportradar (SRAD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SRAD]) and Webtoon Entertainment (WBTN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WBTN]) after they report.
 	* M&A WATCH - Shareholders with Amicus Therapeutics (FOLD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FOLD]) vote on the proposed merger with BioMarin Pharmaceutical (BMRN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BMRN]). Amicus traded about 1.1% below the deal price at the time of publication. Shareholders with Ventyx Biosciences (VTYX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VTYX]) will vote on the buyout offer from Eli Lilly (LLY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LLY]). 
 	* ALL DAY - The four-day CONEXPO-CON/AGG Conference will begin in Las Vegas. The conference is considered the key international gathering for the construction, aggregates, and ready-mixed concrete industries. Widely regarded as the premier international event for the sector, the once-every-three-year gathering also provides a meaningful boost in visitor traffic for major Strip operators, including MGM Resorts (MGM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MGM]), Caesars Entertainment (CZR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CZR]), and Wynn Resorts (WYNN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WYNN]).
 	* 9:55 A.M. Federal Reserve Bank of New York President John Williams will give a keynote address at the America’s Credit Unions Governmental Affairs Conference.
 	* 11:55 A.M. Federal Reserve Bank of Minneapolis President Neel Kashkari will speak at the Bloomberg Invest Conference.

WEDNESDAY - MARCH 4

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Broadcom (AVGO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AVGO]), VEEVA Systems (VEEV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VEEV]), Brown-Forman (BF.A [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BF.A]) (BF.B [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BF.B]), and Okta (OKTA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OKTA]). Options trading implies a double-digit swing in share price for Genius Sports (GENI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GENI]) and StubHub (STUB [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/STUB]) after they report.
 	* ALL DAY - Apple (AAPL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AAPL]) has invited press and creators to in-person "Apple Experience" sessions in New York, London, and Shanghai as part of its coordinated announcements of new iPad/Mac products.
 	* ALL DAY - Lennox (LII [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LII]) will host its Investor Day event in the Dallas-Fort Worth area. The program will feature formal presentations from top management as they outline the company's operational progress, strategic priorities, and new long-term growth targets.
 	* ALL DAY - Pentair (PNR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PNR]) will host its Investor Day event.
 	* 2:00 P.M. The Federal Reserve will release its Beige Book Report. The report will summarize current economic conditions across the Fed's districts.
 	* 4:50 P.M. Netflix (NFLX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NFLX]) CFO Spence Neumann will participate in a Q&A session at the Morgan Stanley Technology, Media & Telecom Conference.

THURSDAY - MARCH 5

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Costco Wholesale (COST [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/COST]), Petrobras (PBR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PBR]), Marvell Technology (MRVL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MRVL]), Kroger (KR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/KR]), JD.com (JD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/JD]), and Guidewire Software (GWRE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GWRE]). Options trading implies double-digit swings for Ciena (CIEN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CIEN]) and Victoria's Secret (VSCO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VSCO]) after they report.
 	* ALL DAY - China's National People's Congress will hold its annual session. The event could signal China's macro policy direction, influencing global markets through targets on GDP growth, fiscal stimulus, and sectors like tech and AI.
 	* 11:00 A.M. Caterpillar (CAT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CAT]) Chief Executive Officer Joe Creed and Construction Industries Group President Rod Shurman are expected to participate in a fireside chat at the CONEXPO Conference in Las Vegas.

FRIDAY - MARCH 6

 	* ALL DAY - The Manheim Used Vehicle Value Index will be released.
 	* ALL DAY - It is the record date for the Booking Holdings 25-for-1 stock split. The company has not executed a stock split since 2003.
 	* ALL DAY - The United Nations will release its latest FAO Food Price Index and monthly grains report, which are watched by commodities traders.
 	* ALL DAY - It is the FDA action date for Bristol Myers Squibb’s (BMY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BMY]) supplemental New Drug Application for Sotyktu (deucravacitinib) to treat adults with active psoriatic arthritis.
 	* 8:30 A.M. The January Retail Sales report will be released by the U.S. Census Bureau.
 	* 8:30 A.M. The February jobs report will be released. Economists expect 62K job additions for the month and for the unemployment rate to remain at 4.4%.

_Seeking Alpha Editor Josh Fineman contributed to this article._

MORE ON THE MARKETS

title: Reddit, KKR, and 2 Biotech IPOs Seeing the Love From Insiders
content:
Four stocks seeing the most notable insider purchases in the past week or so were biotechs SpyGlass Pharma (NASDAQ: SGP) and Veradermics (NYSE: MANE), as well as KKR (NYSE: KKR) and Reddit (NYSE: RDDT).

24/7 Wall St. Key Points

KKR (KKR), Reddit (RDDT), SpyGlass Pharma (SGP), and Veradermics (MANE) saw huge insider buying in the past week. Insider buying can signal management’s confidence in a company’s future prospects. A recent study identified one single habit that doubled Americans’ retirement savings and moved retirement from dream, to reality. Read more here.

Insider buying is generally viewed as a bullish signal. While insiders may sell for many reasons (taxes, diversification, buying a home), they typically only buy for one reason: they believe the price will rise. It can signal management’s confidence in the company’s future prospects or internal turnaround. Furthermore, it often suggests that the stock is undervalued by the market, especially if the purchase is made during a period of price weakness.

Let’s take a quick look at these four transactions.

SpyGlass Pharma

Beneficial owners RA Capital Management and New Enterprise Associates (NEA) participated in a large private placement or follow-on offering. RA Capital added almost 3.7 million shares, while NEA added 937,500 shares, both at the initial public offering price of $16.00 per share.

SpyGlass recently announced positive Phase 2 data for its intraocular drug delivery system for glaucoma. The stock is trading near $28.20, above the IPO price. Analysts are highly bullish, with a Strong Buy consensus rating, citing the potential to disrupt the $5 billion glaucoma market.

Veradermics

Suvretta Capital Management, a 10% owner, purchased 282,700 shares executed in multiple tranches at a weighted average price of approximately $36.50, totaling $10.5 million. Suvretta has been aggressively accumulating shares over the past six months, increasing its stake by nearly 20% in that period.

Veradermics recently received an Overweight rating from Wells Fargo, following the successful enrollment of its Phase 3 trial for molluscum contagiosum. The share price is currently $43.46, meaning the insider is already up about 19% on the trade. Wall Street is positive on the stock.

Reddit

A director purchased 50,500 shares in a range of $139.11 to $150.00 apiece, for a total of almost $7.5 million. This is one of the first major open-market purchases by a director since the IPO lock-up period ended.

Reddit’s stock has been volatile following its recent earnings report, which showed strong user growth but increased R&D spending on AI search tools. The stock is trading at $139.65, within the director’s purchase price range. Analysts remain mostly bullish, focusing on Reddit’s high-margin data licensing deals with LLM developers.

Story Continues

KKR

A director purchased 50,000 shares on February 9, 2026, at prices ranging from $104.36 to $107.10 per share, totaling over $5.2 million. This director has made several million-dollar purchases over the past two years, consistently buying during minor pullbacks.

KKR recently reported record assets under management exceeding $740 billion. The stock has retreated 22.5% in the past month and was last seen changing hands for less than the buyer’s purchase price range. Analyst sentiment is a bullish, as private equity firms benefit from the reopening of the IPO market.

Data Shows One Habit Doubles American’s Savings And Boosts Retirement

Most Americans drastically underestimate how much they need to retire and overestimate how prepared they are. But data shows that people with one habit have more than double the savings of those who don’t.

And no, it’s got nothing to do with increasing your income, savings, clipping coupons, or even cutting back on your lifestyle. It’s much more straightforward (and powerful) than any of that. Frankly, it’s shocking more people don’t adopt the habit given how easy it is.

title: Hair loss pill developer Veradermics raises $256.3M in upsized IPO
content:
[IPO and stock growth graph]
AlexSecret/iStock via Getty Images

Veradermics (MANE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MANE]), a developer of an oral hair loss therapy, has priced its upsized IPO at $17.00 per share, offering 15,077,647 shares, with all shares sold by the company.

The offering is expected to raise about $256.3M in gross proceeds before fees and expenses. The New Haven, Connecticut-based biopharma had previously marketed to sell 13.35M shares for $14 to $16 each.

At the IPO price, Veradermics is valued at around $596M based on the number of shares outstanding disclosed in its filings.

According to the filings, Wellington Management expressed interest in buying up to $30M of shares at the IPO price, while Eli Lilly (LLY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LLY]) indicated it could acquire up to 4.9% of the biotech’s outstanding shares.

The stock is set to begin trading on the New York Stock Exchange on February 4, 2026, under the ticker “MANE,” with the offering expected to close on February 5, 2026. Underwriters also have a 30-day option to buy up to an additional 2,261,647 shares.

Jefferies, Leerink Partners, Citigroup, and Cantor are acting as joint book-running managers for the offering.

Founded in 2019, Veradermics (MANE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MANE]) touts VDPHL01, an oral non-hormonal treatment for pattern hair loss (PHL), as its lead candidate. Designed to deliver a hair growth agent called minoxidil, it is expected to generate topline data from a Phase 2/3 trial for mild-to-moderate PHL in H2 2026.

MORE ON VERADERMICS, INCORPORATED

 	* Veradermics Finalizes $200 Million IPO Target [https://seekingalpha.com/article/4864998-veradermics-finalizes-200-million-ipo-target]
 	* Hair loss pill developer Veradermics seeks up to $534M valuation in U.S. IPO [https://seekingalpha.com/news/4544415-hair-loss-treatment-developer-veradermics-seeks-us-ipo]
 	* Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating on Veradermics, Incorporated [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MANE/ratings/quant-ratings]

title: Hair Loss Biopharma Firm Veradermics Raises $256 Million in IPO
content:
Bloomberg

(Bloomberg) -- Veradermics Inc., a biopharmaceutical firm focused on hair restoration, raised $256.3 million in an upsized US initial public offering priced above the top of its marketed range.

The Longitude Capital-backed company sold about 15 million shares at $17 each, according to a statement Tuesday. The New Haven, Connecticut-based firm had marketed 13.35 million shares for $14 to $16 each.

Most Read from Bloomberg

Chicago Office Tower Sells at an 87% Discount to Pre-Covid Price Congestion Pricing’s Unexpected Winners: Suburban Drivers Forget Free NYC Buses: Just Build 41 Miles of New Subways The Oklahoma Architect Who Turned Kitsch Into Art In Praise of Urban Disorder

At the IPO price, Veradermics has a market value of about $596 million based on the outstanding shares listed in its filings.

Wellington Management had indicated interest in purchasing as much as $30 million in shares at the IPO price, according to the filings. Eli Lilly & Co. also indicated it would take as much as 4.9% of the biotech’s outstanding shares.

The listing kicks off a busy week of IPOs on US exchanges, with eight companies including Veradermics expected to price and trade offerings raising at least $100 million. That’s the highest number of IPOs in a week since November 2021, data compiled by Bloomberg show.

Founded in 2019, the late clinical-stage biopharmaceutical firm is developing its lead therapy to treat mild-to-moderate pattern hair loss, according to its filing. Veradermics’ experimental non-hormonal hair loss treatment is in late stage trials.

Veradermics reported a net loss of $48.1 million for the nine months ended Sept. 30, compared with a net loss of $20.8 million a year earlier, the filings show.

The offering is being led by Jefferies Financial Group Inc., Leerink Partners, Citigroup Inc. and Cantor Fitzgerald. The company’s shares are expected to trade on the New York Stock Exchange under the symbol MANE.

--With assistance from Anthony Hughes.

title: Catalyst Watch: Amazon earnings, IPO blitz, gold swings, and the jobs report
content:
[bad day at wall street]
franckreporter

Welcome to Seeking Alpha's Catalyst Watch - a breakdown of some of next week's actionable events that stand out. Read more about the events that may impact stock prices next week.

MONDAY - FEBRUARY 2

 	* VOLATILITY WATCH - Coinbase Global (COIN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/COIN]) and Reddit (RDDT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RDDT]) are set up for a volatile week of trading based on options volume. The most overbought stocks per their 14-day relative strength index include Goldsky Resources (GSKRF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GSKRF]), Amerigo Resources (ARREF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ARREF]), and Precipitate Gold (PREIF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PREIF]). The most oversold stocks per their 14-day Relative Strength Index include Roper (ROP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ROP]), Commerce.com (CMRC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CMRC]), and Flutter Entertainment (FLUT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FLUT]). Traders also expect more outsized swings for spot gold (XAUUSD:CUR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XAUUSD:CUR]) (GLD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GLD]) and silver (XAGUSD:CUR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/XAGUSD:CUR]) (SLV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SLV]). Warner Bros Discovery (WBD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WBD]) and Netflix (NFLX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NFLX]) could be active with the Senate antitrust hearing set. 
 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Palantir (PLTR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PLTR]), Disney (DIS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/DIS]), NXP Semiconductors (NXPI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NXPI]), Tyson Foods (TSN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TSN]), Rambus (RMBS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RMBS]), and Teradyne (TER [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TER]).
 	* IPO WATCH - A busy week for IPOs will see the trading debuts for VeraDermics (MANE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MANE]), Once Upon a Farm (OFRM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OFRM]), Eikon Therapeutics (EIKN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/EIKN]), Bob's Discount Furniture (BOBS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BOBS]), Liftoff Mobile (LFTO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LFTO]), and Forgent Power Solutions (FPS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FPS]). The IPO lockup period expires for blocks of shares of Firefly Aerospace (FLY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FLY]).
 	* DIVIDEND WATCH - Companies that have an ex-dividend date coming next week include Citigroup (C [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/C]), MetLife (MET [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MET]), Valero (VLO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VLO]), and JB Hunt (JBHT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/JBHT]).
 	* OIL WATCH - Oil futures could be active after eight OPEC+ nations reviewed the decision to pause oil production increases in Q1.
 	* ALL DAY - Lululemon (LULU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LULU]) CFO Meghan Frank and Chief Commercial Officer André Maestrini will start to share leadership duties as co-CEOs while the company continues its search for a full-time replacement for Calvin McDonald.
 	* ALL DAY - The National Automobile Dealers Association’s annual four-day convention in Las Vegas begins. The event brings together dealers, manufacturers, and technology vendors. Marchex (MCHX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MCHX]), Faraday Future Intelligent Electric (FFAI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FFAI]), and Pinewood.AI (PINWF [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PINWF]) are some of the companies participating.
 	* 2:00 P.M. The Federal Reserve's Senior Loan Officer Opinion Survey on Bank Lending Practices will be released. The SLOOS report will provide an update on the conditions, supply and demand of bank loans extended to customers in the U.S. Tightening standards can signal stress in the banking system or broader economy, impacting financial stocks.

TUESDAY - FEBRUARY 3

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include AMD (AMD [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMD]), Merck (MRK [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MRK]), PepsiCo (PEP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PEP]), Amgen (AMGN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMGN]), Suncor Energy (SU [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SU]), Mondelez International (MDLZ [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MDLZ]), Electronic Arts (EA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/EA]), PayPal (PYPL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PYPL]), and Chipotle (CMG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CMG]). Options trading implies a double-digit swing in share price for Galaxy Digital (GLXY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GLXY]) and fuboTV (FUBO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/FUBO]) after they report.
 	* ALL DAY - Western Digital Corporation (WDC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/WDC]) will hold its Innovation Day event in New York City. At the event, the company will highlight key innovations designed to support the growing demand for data storage by customers in the AI-driven data economy.
 	* ALL DAY - Shareholders with Semrush (SEMR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SEMR]) vote on the acquisition offer from Adobe (ADBE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ADBE]). Semrush traded at less than 1% from the deal price at the time of publication.
 	* 8:15 A.M. PepsiCo (PEP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PEP]) will hold its earnings conference call. Key topics will include the guidance update, organic revenue and volume trends, North America snacks and beverages elasticity, and execution on the new shareholder-value plan. Key suppliers Celsius Holdings (CELH [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CELH]) and McCormick (MKC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MKC]) trade directionally with PepsiCo about 75% of the time after earnings.
 	* 10:30 A.M. The two-day Oppenheimer Emerging Growth Conference will begin. Aeva Technologies (AEVA [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AEVA]), GrowGeneration (GRWG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GRWG]), and Aehr Test Systems (AEHR [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AEHR]) are some of the notable companies participating.

WEDNESDAY - FEBRUARY 4

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Alphabet (GOOG [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GOOG]), Eli Lilly (LLY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/LLY]), AbbVie (ABBV [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ABBV]), Uber Technologies (UBER [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/UBER]), and Qualcomm (QCOM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/QCOM]). Options trading implies a double-digit swing in share price for Boot Barn (BOOT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BOOT]) and Snap (SNAP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SNAP]) after they report.
 	* ALL DAY - Shareholders with Noodles & Company (NDLS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/NDLS]) vote on a proposed reverse stock split.
 	* ALL DAY - General Motors (GM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GM]) CFO Paul Jacobson will give comments at the Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago Automotive Insights Symposium.
 	* POSTMARKET - Costco (COST [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/COST]) will issue its January sales report.

THURSDAY - FEBRUARY 5

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Amazon (AMZN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMZN]), Shell (SHEL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/SHEL]), ConocoPhillips (COP [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/COP]), Bristol-Myers Squibb (BMY [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/BMY]), Barrick Mining (B [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/B]), and ArcelorMittal (MT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MT]). Options trading implies double-digit swings for Reddit (RDDT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RDDT]), Gold.com (Gold [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/GOLD]), and Roblox (RBLX [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/RBLX]) after they report.
 	* ALL DAY - American Axle & Manufacturing (AXL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AXL]) will begin trading under its new name and symbol of Dauch Corporation (DCH). The name change is tied to the transformational strategic milestone of its planned acquisition of Dowlais Group and to reposition the business under a broader, less product-specific corporate identity.
 	* ALL DAY - Shareholders with Veeco Instruments (VECO [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/VECO]) and Axcelis Technologies (ACLS [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ACLS]) vote on the planned merger between the companies.
 	* 7:45 A.M. The European Central Bank will release its policy statement.
 	* 8:30 A.M. European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde will hold a press conference.
 	* 10:50 A.M. Atlanta Federal Reserve Bank President Raphael Bostic will participate in a moderated conversation and Q&A on monetary policy.
 	* 1:30 P.M. St. Louis Federal Reserve Bank President Alberto Musalem will speak on the U.S. economy and monetary policy and participate in a moderated discussion before the University of Arkansas Center for Business and Economic Research.
 	* 5:00 P.M. Amazon (AMZN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AMZN]) will hold its earnings conference call. Key topics will include AWS growth and competitive positioning, how investors should view surging AI-driven capex, and 2026 investment plans that could include OpenAI (OPENAI [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/OPENAI]). Suppliers that have a high trading correlation with Amazon on earnings day include Hamilton Beach (HBB [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/HBB]), Marvell Technology (MRVL [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MRVL]), and Aterian (ATER [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/ATER]).

FRIDAY - FEBRUARY 6

 	* EARNINGS WATCH - Notable companies due to report include Toyota Motor (TM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/TM]), Philip Morris (PM [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/PM]), Centene (CNC [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/CNC]), and Autonation (AN [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/AN]).
 	* ALL DAY - Immunovant (IMVT [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/IMVT]) will hold a business update call.
 	* 8:30 A.M. The January jobs report will be released. Economists expect 65K job additions for the month and for the unemployment rate to remain at 4.4%.

title: Hair loss pill developer Veradermics seeks up to $534M valuation in U.S. IPO
content:
[IPO and stock growth graph]
AlexSecret

Veradermics (MANE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MANE]), a developer of an oral hair loss therapy, has filed to sell 13.4M shares of its common stock in its U.S. initial public offering under the ticker symbol “MANE,” seeking a valuation of up to $534M.

In an SEC filing on Wednesday, the New Haven, Connecticut-based biopharma announced its IPO plans, which it said will generate nearly $181.8M in net proceeds at the midpoint of the $14-$16 per share in the marketed range.

The company could command a valuation of $533.6M at the top end of the range, assuming roughly 33.4M shares outstanding immediately after the offering, excluding the underwriters’ option to purchase nearly 2.0M additional shares.

Founded in 2019, Veradermics (MANE [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MANE]) touts VDPHL01, an oral non-hormonal treatment for pattern hair loss (PHL), as its lead candidate. Designed to [https://edgar.secdatabase.com/1467/162828026003764/filing-main.htm] deliver a hair growth agent called minoxidil, it is expected to generate topline data from a Phase 2/3 trial for mild-to-moderate PHL in H2 2026.

MORE ON VERADERMICS, INC

 	* Seeking Alpha’s Quant Rating on VeraDermics, Inc [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MANE/ratings/quant-ratings]
 	* Financial information for VeraDermics, Inc [https://seekingalpha.com/symbol/MANE/income-statement]
